title: BERT's Masked Language Modeling
concept: BERT is trained by hiding random words (e.g. 15% mask) and predicting them using context from both left and right directions.

exercises.prompt: How does the BERT AI model practice understanding human language?

options.text: By translating sentences to another language
By hiding words and guessing them using both left and right context
By deleting all nouns from the dictionary

exercises.explain: BERT stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, and it is famous for looking at context in both directions (left and right).

cards.title: Play Fill-in-the-Blanks!
cards.body: Have you ever played a game of fill-in-the-blanks? It turns out that hiding words is the secret recipe that makes one of the world's smartest AI systems, BERT, understand human language so well!

cards.title: Masked Language Modeling
cards.body: Masked Language Modeling (MLM) is a training trick where we hide (or "mask") random words in a sentence—usually about 15% of them. BERT is then challenged to guess those hidden words by looking at the remaining words on both sides.

cards.title: The Smudged Ink Trick
cards.body: Imagine reading a sentence where someone has spilled ink over a few words. To guess the missing words, you don't just look at what came before the smudge; you also read the words after the smudge to get the full context. That's exactly how BERT learns!

cards.title: Guessing the Mask
cards.body:
If we feed BERT the sentence: "The [MASK] chased the mouse."
BERT looks at "The" (left context) and "chased the mouse" (right context) to predict that the hidden word [MASK] is likely "cat" or "dog".

cards.title: It's Not a One-Way Street
cards.body: Some people think BERT only reads sentences from left to right like we do. In reality, BERT reads the entire sentence in both directions at the same time, using clues from before and after the hidden word to make its guess!

cards.title: How BERT Sees Sentences
cards.body: Traditional models only look in one direction, while BERT looks at both sides of a hidden word at the same time.

left.text: Can only read from left-to-right, predicting the very next word.

right.text: Reads left-to-right and right-to-left to guess the hidden [MASK] word.

exercises.prompt: To guess a hidden word, what context does BERT look at?

options.text: Only the words that come before the mask
Only the very first and last words of the sentence
Words from both the left and the right of the mask
Only dictionary definitions of the words

exercises.explain: BERT stands out because it is bidirectional, meaning it uses context from both the left and the right sides of the hidden word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: BERT trains by hiding every single word in a sentence.
exercises.explain: BERT hides only a small portion (typically 15%) of the words, not all of them, so it has enough surrounding context to make a guess.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the training percentage for BERT's masking.
exercises.sentence: During training, BERT usually hides about [blank] of the words in a sentence.
exercises.explain: BERT typically hides around 15% of the words in a sentence during its training to practice guessing them from context.

exercises.prompt: Which model is using BERT's Masked Language Modeling style?

options.text: Model A: "The cat sat on the [MASK] (reads only left-to-right)"
Model B: "The [MASK] sat on the mat (reads both left and right context)"
Model C: "mat the on sat cat [MASK] (reads only right-to-left)"

exercises.explain: AI Model B is using BERT's bidirectional masking technique. Model A is unidirectional (reading only left-to-right), and Model C is just reading backwards.

exercises.prompt: You are building a custom language model and want to train it using BERT's signature Masked Language Modeling (MLM) technique. How does this training process work?

options.text: It reads sentences only from left to right to guess the very last word.
It hides random words and uses both left and right context clues to guess them.
It replaces every single word with a mask so the model has to guess the entire sentence from scratch.
It blocks out punctuation marks so the model learns where to put periods and commas.

exercises.explain: BERT's Masked Language Modeling (MLM) randomly hides words (often 15%) and forces the AI to look at both the left and right context to predict the missing words, preventing it from cheating by just looking forward.
